titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Greenwood Plantation and Greenwood Seed Company records
Greenwood Plantation and Greenwood Seed Company records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Rachel L. Mihalko

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 20, 2021 at 07:22

did.unittitle: Greenwood Plantation and Greenwood Seed Company records

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open to the Yale community for research and teaching. Other researchers require the written permission of the Greentree Foundation to access the collection prior to January 1, 2050, after which the papers are open for research without restriction. Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives website.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in seven series: I. Business and Real Estate Files, 1938-2000. II. Financial Records, 1941-1995. III. Property Records, Insurance, and Title Documentation, 1918-2002. IV. Correspondence, 1934-1999. V. Photographs, Albums, and Guest Books, 1886-1999. VI. Greenwood Seed Company, 1946-1992. VII. Publicity, 1906-1999.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Greenwood Plantation comprises three non-contiguous tracts of land: Home Place; Mitchell-Swift; and Boston Farm. The smallest tract, Home Place, was designated as a National Historic Register site in 1976 and contains the main house, outbuildings, gardens, the hybrid seed company, and Big Woods, a rare forest of old-growth long-leaf pines.

Payne Whitney acquired the land in 1916 from his uncle, Colonel Oliver Hazard Payne, who purchased it in 1899. The collection includes photographs of staff who resided on the plantation at the time of purchase, and Whitney family members who resided on the property. John Hay Whitney inherited the property in 1944 with his wife, Betsey Cushing Roosevelt, and they lived in the Plantation's main house. While under the ownership of John Hay Whitney, the Home Place tract served as a weekend home for family gatherings and other invited guests. Greenwood Plantation lands were also utilized for hybrid corn seed experimentation, timber experiments, and other agricultural studies under the management of Edward V. Komarek. Komarek introduced the practice of controlled burning of the forest floor and selective cutting of timber to support preservation and propagation of the woods.
Betsey Cushing Whitney left the Greenwood Plantation properties to The Greentree Foundation, the Whitney family's philanthropic organization.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Greenwood Plantation and Greenwood Seed Company records (MS 2089). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection comprises the records of Greenwood Plantation and Greenwood Seed Company of Thomasville, Georgia. It includes documentation of hybrid corn seed production, business records, finances associated with farm properties and employees, legal records compiled by the Whitney estate, and correspondence. The correspondence contains frequent farm reports to John Hay Whitney, mainly from farm manager E. V. Komarek, summarizing activities on the farm, including crop production, timber, sales, game management, staff behavior, vehicle issues, and property maintenance. The records also include photographs of the Whitney family and activities that occurred at the plantation, including quail and turkey hunting, horse racing, and water activities by Heard's Pond. The records also contain materials related to a fire at the property in 1993, including witness testimony and insurance papers compiled by Betsey Cushing Whitney.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Reproductions in any form of this material require the written permission of the Greentree Foundation until January 1, 2050. After January 1, 2050, reproduction is permitted but subject to applicable copyright laws. Copyright for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Greenwood Plantation and Greenwood Seed Company was transferred to Yale University in 2019. These materials may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact mssa.assist@yale.edu. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exexclusively with the user.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Tribune Association stock certificates, Volume II, was moved from this collection to Series I of MS 1938, John Hay Whitney and Betsey Cushing Whitney family papers.

did.unittitle: Memorandum appointing Louis A. Beard as manager of Greenwood Plantation
Inventory for equipment and land valuation
Business correspondence, bills, checks, memorandum, notes, staffing, and farm reports
Controlled burning and lake photographs, brochures, and correspondence
Correspondence regarding vehicles at Greenwood Plantation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related organizations include organizations with which E. V. Komarek appears to have been involved, as well as research stations that may have collaborated with Greenwood Plantation on experimenting with hybrid seed corn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping comprises information about the institute, including correspondence, membership lists, annual meeting notes and schedules, by-laws, and brochures.

did.unittitle: National Academy of Science's National Research Council

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains an invitation to serve on the Agricultural Research Institute Governing Board and E. V. Komarek's acceptance.

did.unittitle: First Annual Tall Timbers Fire Ecology Conference program
Preliminary sketches of proposed research station building
Financial report, correspondence, photographs, news clippings, and survey maps

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping comprises documentation related to timber management at Greenwood Plantation, including bids, contracts, correspondence, sales invoices, tax information, and timber reports prepared by E. V. Komarek and other forestry consultants.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains timber maps for Boston Farm (1955), Mitchell-Swift property (1955), Boston Farm (1955-1958) annotated, corn acreage at Boston Farm (1965), Home Place property annotated for crop years 1978-1979, Boston Farm annotated from 1983-1985, and a harvest map for May 1985.

did.unittitle: A Guide to Federal Income Tax for Timber Owners, U.S. Department of Agriculture Handbook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping includes sales statements, bids for timber, receipts, timber collection reports, checks from Leon Neel and Associates, and timber contracts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Timber reports contain volume growth statistics, forest management summaries, and timber sales documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Harold Sebring was a consulting forester and sawmill operator who worked in South Carolina on forest management plans and timber appraisals.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This grouping is arranged in alphabetical order by correspondent name, and chronological order within correspondent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping includes farm-related correspondence maintained by E. V. Komarek. Correspondents include John Hay Whitney, Samuel C. Park, Louis A. Beard, Charles Frank Deshler, and others working in the farm industry. Topics range from farm reports to Louis A. Beard and John Hay Whitney summarizing daily activities such as hunting, timber, labor, and crops, and correspondence related to hybrid seed corn and agricultural committees. Other topics include expense reports, financial concerns, and insight on hiring and wages to Samuel C. Park and Charles Frank Deshler. He also writes about his project outline and vision for the plantation from when he first assumed a leadership role.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping includes hunting agreements, expense and balance sheets for farm operations, and payroll information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping includes John Hay Whitney's correspondence with E. V. Komarek, Louis A. Beard, and other farm staff. The letters include summaries of farm operations for Whitney while he was serving as American ambassador at the United Kingdom Embassy, plans to visit the farm, and financial matters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping comprises annual, monthly, and mid-year farm operations reports, mostly written by E. V. Komarek to John Hay Whitney.

did.unittitle: Soil Associations of the United States

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Map and key created by the United States Department of Agriculture, Soil Survey Division.

did.unittitle: Greenwood hybrid corn brochure and photographs of meetings
Monthly reports of hybrid corn seed sales and stock
Florida Corn , book and letters from recipients
Seed sales, planting schedules, and related correspondence
Moews Seed Company, seed orders and correspondence
National Council of Commercial Plant Breeders, meeting notes and by-laws
Patent on seed production from the American Seed Trade Association Inc., United States Patent Office
Corn seed patents, including Jones Patent for a seed production study
Future Farmers of America (FFA) Greenwood Corn Program

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This program consisted of educational youth programs in which Greenwood Seed Corn was donated.

did.unittitle: Memoranda from Samuel C. Park regarding hybrid corn
Agricultural Stabilization & Conservation Service (ASC), notices of allotment and yields for corn
Agricultural Stabilization & Conservation Service (ASC), annotated map of Home Place property
Corn production, land reports, game program, and historical data from 1866-1974
Planting plans, landscape designs, and blueprints
Planting plans, landscape designs, and blueprints
Report written by T. A. Liefield for work done on hog pasture
Quail reports and quail issue map
Quail issue map for Boston Farm
Quail summaries, dog strings, and hunting
Nilo Plantation Quail Report from John Merrill Olin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a chart of names, dates, game, and remarks about shooting conditions.

did.unittitle: Game program, summary with map of Home Place property

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains memoranda regarding tung oil experiments and correspondence with Frank King, George King, and Byron Southwell, workers at the experiment station.

did.unittitle: Correspondence regarding experiment stations and hybrid seed corn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes correspondence with Frederick D. Richey, agronomist at U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Experiment Station in Knoxville, Tennessee, and Wayne Freeman, corn breeder of Greenwood Farms and Greenwood Seed Company.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged chronologically within each grouping.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series comprises materials related to bookkeeping for Greenwood Plantation farm employees, sales and accounts, operating costs, and tax documents, including records associated with Greenwood Seed Company.

did.unittitle: Account ledger pages, balance sheets, and correspondence
Spencer Chemical Company, invoices for fertilizer and correspondence
Greenwood Seed Company report on accounting procedures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes handwritten notes by John Hay Whitney detailing costs of farm operations (wages, seeds, feed, gas, property, utilities, and insurance) from 1938-1965, written in 1965.

did.unittitle: Deshler, Charles Frank, reports and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping comprises records of Charles Frank Deshler, including correspondence regarding Greenwood Seed Company, farm budgeting, and payroll information.

did.unittitle: Reports and letters to E. V. Komarek
Correspondence regarding Fair Labor Standards Act and seed sales
Reports and memos from E. V. Komarek
Employment contract for Wayne H. Freeman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes a biographical sketch of Wayne Freeman, who was responsible for corn breeding, and his employment confirmation.

did.unittitle: Travel expenses for E. V. Komarek
Greenwood Seed Company employee files and payroll information
Group insurance plan from the Soil Conservation Society of America
Wages, worksheets, and typed sheets regarding farmhands and agricultural laborers
Komarek, E. V., accounting and payroll notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes financial reports prepared for Greenwood Seed Company for each fiscal year, from 1946-1959; reports for 1969, 1974, and 1975 are not included.

did.unittitle: Highway map of US 19/84/319 for by-pass through main house

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files are maintained in the order in which they were received.

Ledger of expenses compiled for auditing

Board of Tax Assessors, notices and correspondence
Board of Tax Assessors, tax bills

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes an assessment notice and correspondence regarding an appeal for ad valorem tax valuation of Greenwood property.

did.unittitle: Georgia Tax Reform Commission working papers
Tax records maintained by Greenwood Seed Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files are maintained in the order in which they were received.

did.unittitle: Intangible tax returns, Greenwood Seed Company
Georgia intangible property tax, Greenwood Seed Company
Tangible personal property, Greenwood Seed Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records include United States Partnership Return of Income, Form 1065 and New York income and Unincorporated Business Tax Partnership forms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records include United States Partnership Return of Income, Form 1065 and New York income and Unincorporated Business Tax Partnership forms.

Georgia Corporation income tax, Seed Development Company

Georgia sales and use tax, Seed Development Company

Foreign corporation report, Seed Development Company
Georgia corporation information return, Seed Development Company

Greenwood Seed Company, John Hay Whitney
United States corporation income tax, including surtax allocation
United States corporation income tax, including Whitney Communications Corporation surtax allocation
Georgia property tax, including John Hay Whitney, correspondence
Georgia property tax, including John Hay Whitney, oversize tax returns
Georgia property tax, including John Hay Whitney ad valorem tax, correspondence
Georgia property tax, including John Hay Whitney ad valorem tax, oversize tax returns
Georgia income and occupation or license, form 600
Georgia sales and use tax division, application for registration
Property Records, Insurance, and Title Documentation

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: General files are arranged in chronological order.

Right of way deeds, real estate appraisal, property inventory and ownership information

Property records, distribution of the estate of Louis A. Beard
Greenwood Seed Company stock certificates and stockholder names

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes correspondence to Betsey Cushing Whitney.

Greenwood Plantation assessed valuations, timber maps
Real estate, deeds, right of way, and correspondence

Court documents regarding road relocation, Thomasville by-pass right of way

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes deeds, statements, and correspondence compiled by the law offices of Alexander and Vann.

did.unittitle: Real estate and correspondence regarding property and land
Greenwood Lodge post-fire finances and contractor agreements
Greenwood Lodge, state insurance appraisals, photographs, and correspondence with John S. Hand, Architect
Greenwood Lodge floorplans for proposed addition and alterations
Real estate, leases, purchases, offers to buy
Whitney family hunting weekends at Greenwood Lodge

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes memoranda about scheduling weekends for family members to stay at Greenwood Lodge.

did.unittitle: Legal files from Sherman & Sterling regarding the sale of Mitchell Swift property in 1999
Correspondence related to purchase offers from real estate agents
Drafts for Mitchell Swift property sale
Property inventory, ownership history, and the estate of Betsey Cushing Whitney

Executor's deed of assent for Heard's Pond, including correspondence
Property Acquisition Committee of the Georgia Trust meetings
Georgia Trust Proposal for the Cultural Education and Conservation Center at Greenwood
Insurance appraisal for Home Place property prepared by Firemen's Fund
Rose Leaves , publication featuring articles on Greenwood
Greenwood Plantation, historical data: photographs, property deeds, negatives, correspondence
Thesis on the Greenwood Plantation in Thomasville, Georgia, 1827-1962, with emphasis on the owners, by Charles Thomas Hill, student attending Valdosta State College
Correspondence with the Nature Conservancy and the Department of Natural Resources regarding Greenwood nomination to National Register of Historic Places

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains research materials, including correspondence, notes, photographs, and history of land ownership.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, historic reports, and National Register of Historic Places nomination
National Register of Historic Places, 1975 to 1976, form and information
Georgia State Archives correspondence with E. V. Komarek regarding access to archives
Research for John Hay Whitney about Greenwood and family history

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes three small booklets on Piney Woods Hotel, an article on Johns Hopkins, and E. V. Komarek's research on timber.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes personal correspondence to Betsey Cushing Whitney and newspaper clippings about Greenwood Plantation and Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis.

did.unittitle: Memoranda and correspondence about Greenwood Plantation's history

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes the Nature Conservancy and Georgia Trust Report "Archaeological Investigations of the Potential Sites of a Cemetery and Three Barracks within Greenwood Plantation" by Garrow & Associates, December 1992.

did.unittitle: Greenwood Plantation history and press clippings
Printed articles about the plantation, historical report, and research on John Hay and Joan Payson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a letter from Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis to Betsey Cushing Whitney about a visit to Greenwood and photocopies of Oliver Payne correspondence with Stanford White.

did.unittitle: Greenwood Plantation history, miscellaneous articles and maps

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping includes files compiled by Betsey Cushing Whitney's estate to prepare for property transfers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The files are maintained in the order in which they were received, which reflects their order as arranged by the estate.

did.unittitle: Correspondence regarding appraisal of property, attachments
Sale of Home Place property to Greentree Foundation indenture and certification

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Table of contents: accounting/bookkeeping, land/building rental, hunting season, employee information, review of bills, and pictures.

Listing agreement between Chubb Associates and Robert Carswell
Drafts and marked copies of estate availability and listing agreement
Proposed schedule for auction of Mitchell-Swift property, draft

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping comprises copies of John Hay Whitney's farm records and Betsey Cushing Whitney's correspondence, including documentation of financial transactions, farm reports, tax declarations, leases, farm memoranda, and National Register correspondence.

Historic structure reports compiled as research for Greenwood Plantation historic structure report

Robert W. Woodruff Foundation, volume II

Materials regarding the National Register of Historic Places

Timber Report for Boston Farm, by Earl H. Bennett
Timber Report for Home Place and Heard's Pond properties, by Earl H. Bennett
Timber Report for Mitchell-Swift property, by Earl H. Bennett
Singletary field contract of sale drafts
Sale of singletary field, 30 acres sold to Emmett "Buck" Mitchell III
Contract for land easement on Mitchell-Swift property

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to Betsey Cushing Whitney from the National Trust for Historic Preservation, the Nature Conservancy, and the National Park Service, compiled by Shearman and Sterling, on behalf of Betsey Cushing Whitney, to plan for the preservation of Greenwood.

did.unittitle: Georgia Trust for Historic Preservation, Home Place property preservation plan
Architect contract with John S. Hand of Thomasville, Georgia for Greenwood renovation
Architect contract, preliminary drawings for proposed addition
Documentation on the historical, cultural, and environmental heritage of Greenwood Plantation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file also includes resumes and applications from architects.

did.unittitle: Meeting with George Paxton from the Georgia Trust for Historic Preservation and Carrier & Associates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes information packets mailed to panelists participating in a market study for Greenwood Plantation.

did.unittitle: Hunting, fishing, and timber agency agreement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes indentures for property transfer from John Hay Whitney's will.

did.unittitle: Maps of Thomasville area, Boston Farm, Home Place, and Mitchell-Swift properties
Survey map of Heard's Pond property
One Beekman Place, New York City
Right of way easement to Oglethorpe Power Corporation
Right of way easement to Southern Bell Telephone
Material regarding the closing of Millpond Road

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes Department of Transportation pamphlets and notice of bypass construction.

did.unittitle: Drafts of easement to Southern Bell Telephone and Telegraph Company
Greenwood by-pass files from the law offices of Alexander and Vann
Notes on pension plans, personnel names, meeting minutes, and correspondence about right of way easement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping comprises records documenting a 1993 fire and the subsequent exterior restoration of the main house. This restoration won an award for outstanding exterior restoration from the Georgia Trust for Historic Preservation in 1998.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The bulk of the materials in this grouping is arranged chronologically. Some materials within this grouping are arranged in the order in which they were received.

did.unittitle: Heard's Pond property, Nature Conservancy's report
United States Department of Agriculture farm reports
News broadcast of the Greenwood fire, six hour videorecording

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This grouping is arranged in the order in which it was received.

did.unittitle: Witness statements, loss reports, and condolences
Interviews with staff, inventories, and photographs of fire
Statements and interviews regarding the fire
Transcripts of interviews with witnesses and plantation staff
Statements and interviews with witnesses with index
Fireman's Fund Insurance Company survey report, building photos and maps, and working papers
Soil remediation, removal of waste, and other forms from the United States Environmental Protection Agency
Greenwood Plantation tax filing for Georgia state taxes
The Georgia Trust for Historic Preservation certificate to Greenwood Plantation for outstanding exterior restoration
Whitney dwelling loss, tape from Frank Crystal Personal Insurance

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

did.unittitle: Items damaged in fire and restoration of fine arts

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This grouping is arranged in the order in which it was received.

did.unittitle: Items damaged in fire, photographs, and memoranda
Photographs of paintings, artworks, and objects restored at Williamstown Regional Art Conservation Laboratory (WRACL) in Williamstown, Massachusetts
Documentary color slides of damaged artwork and correspondence with conservator
Photographs and slides from Williamstown Regional Art Conservation Laboratory (WRACL) of artwork before, during, and after treatment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains pre-fire furniture restoration records.

Invoices for household appliances and cleaning services

Items taken to Georgia or replacement items

Hooped and needlepoint rugs from Betsey Cushing Whitney's office
Silver, china, glassware, and small artworks
Greenwood home interior and photographs of the fire
Furniture, pre-fire photographs from the Georgia Trust
News clippings, thank you notes from visitors, and articles about the fire

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Includes correspondence regarding the Georgia Trust for Historic Preservation certificate to Greenwood Plantation for exterior restoration (box 60, folder 5)

did.unittitle: Ledger reports for renovation after fire
Greenwood Plantation architectural history reports, copies of photographs with index, and renovation proposal by John S. Hand
Floorplans from Greenwood Plantation architectural history report
Boston Lodge renovation recommendations from John Hand, architect
Henry H. Lewis contractors for the exterior restoration check vouchers and invoices
A Plan for the Preservation and Conservation of Greenwood Plantation's Home Place property, executive summary and correspondence
A Plan for the Preservation and Conservation of Greenwood Plantation's Home Place property
Historic structure reports for Greenwood Plantation

Georgia Trust for Historic Preservation structural reports
Main house at Greenwood Plantation, prepared for the Georgia Trust for Historic Preservation by John G. Waite, architect

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This grouping is arranged as received from the donor.

did.unittitle: Greenwood Disclaimed Properties Committee, press clipping binder
Greenwood Disclaimed Properties Committee, Home Place property
Greenwood Disclaimed Properties Committee, Assetts Report for Greentree Foundation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes blueprints for all Greenwood Plantation properties: Home Place, Heard's Pond, Boston Farm, and Mitchell-Swift. Plot plans were drawn by Francis C. Carr and Co., Inc., 1936. Other architects include Umberto Innocenti and Richard K. Webel, who created blueprints for John Hay Whitney in 1940 and 1954.

did.unittitle: Greenwood Plantation land and property maps
Georgia-Florida Field Trial Club map of ownership
Home Place and Heard's Pond properties

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes a land survey made for Payne Whitney, maps of all properties from Harold Sebring, Umberto Innocenti's staking plan for service court, a topographic map, and an aerial survey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series comprises incoming and outgoing correspondence related to farm activities, events, related associations, clients, employees, and agricultural topics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains personal letters to the Whitneys from guests at Greenwood Plantation, letters regarding E. V. Komarek's travel in Africa, and agriculture-related letters from E. V. Komarek to John Hay Whitney.

did.unittitle: Maps of shooting courses from general correspondence
Tift and Stoddard letters to Louis A. Beard
Warburton, Frank W., regarding farm vehicles
Good Hope from Jamaica regarding the import of hybrid corn
Richey, Dr. Frederick D. of Agricultural Experiment Station, Tennessee
General Motors Corporation exploring a shooting car for purchase

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes a photograph of a 1949 car.

did.unittitle: Kleberg, Robert J., Jr., King Ranch livestock and crops
Thomasville Airport charter information and proposed land lease for Campbell Airport
Greenwood Farms correspondence regarding insurance, taxes, and leases
Selvage & Lee, letters regarding event expenses
Komarek, Roy, letter to Massey-Harris Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Massey-Harris Company manufactured self-propelled corn pickers.

did.unittitle: Florida Power and Light Company map, sketch showing approximate location of proposed 66kv single pole electric line, with property contract attached
Innocenti, Umberto, landscape architect, correspondence, plans, blueprints, and expense sheets
WKTG, CBS Television and Southern Broadcasting System on creating a Thomasville television station
Talmadge, Herman E., governor of Georgia
Thomasville Garden Club Inc. regarding garden statuary
Georgia State Chamber of Commerce, letter appointing E. V. Komarek to Agriculture Division
Humphrey, G. M. on forming a committee of conservationists
Richey, Dr. Frederick D. and American Seed Trade Association
Committee of Commercial Corn Breeders meeting minutes, by-laws, and correspondence
Hybrid Corn Division of Southern Seedsmen's Association, member correspondence regarding corn allotments
Agricultural Extension Service regarding Greenwood Corn Bulletin
Conservation Workshop at Florida State University
Florida State Department of Education correspondence regarding farm partnerships with vocational schools

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes photographs from a 1961 corn production banquet.

did.unittitle: Generals' annual visits, telegrams and letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes correspondence and planning for an annual event in which United States military generals were invited to stay at Greenwood Plantation.

did.unittitle: Wellhausen, Dr. E. J., Rockefeller Foundation
Brazilian Bulletin , with a note to E. V. Komarek
Robertson, William B., Jr., regarding controlled burning
Shoemaker, Jack, regarding Florida corn booklet reprints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes a lease from Greenwood Seed Company, a bill of sale, and stocks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Campbell Street Airport was purchased by John Hay Whitney to give to the city of Thomasville. Correspondence is related to a proposal to convert the grounds into an industrial park.

did.unittitle: Bowen, Ross, production agreement for Greenwood Seed Company
Greenwood House postcard and pension and payroll accounting
Balfour Field, correspondence about timber and forest issues
West Pinetree Boulevard paving project correspondence
Georgia traffic map and state highway system
Map of Thomasville and Thomas County, Georgia
Thomas County Association for Retarded Children, Inc., youth camp
Agricultural Weather Service, United States Weather Bureau conference notes and agendas, research notes, correspondence
Agricultural Weather Service, United States Weather Bureau correspondence
Greenwood Plantation correspondence regarding the main house
Georgia Trust for Historic Preservation and Thomas Company Historical Society, donation requests

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes correspondence regarding payroll and staffing, thank you notes from guests, and monthly farm reports to Betsey Cushing Whitney.

did.unittitle: Georgia Trust for Historic Preservation Inc. correspondence with Betsey Cushing Whitney
Memoranda and letters to Terry Blanchard regarding plantation expenses, sales, press, and operating rules
Hadley, Albert, happy birthday folder to Betsey Cushing Whitney
AON Risk Services pollution claim correspondence
Big Woods, building exteriors and flowers
Boston and Mitchell Place properties, woods
Burning and aerial photographs, Tall Timbers
Heard's Pond property and surrounding areas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes images of groups of people working and color transparencies, probably shot for advertising purposes.

did.unittitle: Landscape and nature at Greenwood Plantation
Livestock, horse carriages, unidentified people, and a house at Greenwood Plantation
Property views of the main house

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These photographs were taken in 1886 when the property was owned by the Jones family, and in 1936 when the Whitney family owned the property.

did.unittitle: Activities and guests at Greenwood Plantation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs document horseback riding, hunting, grilling, and canoeing at Greenwood Plantation. Guests pictured are Whitney and di Bonaventura family members and friends, including Fred Astaire and Alvin "Shipwreck" Kelly.

did.unittitle: Albums containing photographs of parties and hunting

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes photographs of John Hay Whitney shooting quail with Dwight D. (Dwight David) Eisenhower, and of individuals rowing in a lake.

did.unittitle: Shooting party with John Hay Whitney
Portrait with Mamie Dowd Eisenhower, Greenwood front porch

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two images (TIFF and JPG) of the Eisenhowers facing forward.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes photographs of an annual event in which United States military generals were invited to stay at Greenwood Plantation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes some photographs with identifying names on the verso.

did.unittitle: Guests at Greenwood Plantation and newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a snapshot of Fred Astaire and Alvin "Shipwreck" Kelly dancing at Greenwood.

did.unittitle: Guests, views from inside the house, and pictures of hunting

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes photographs of Dwight D. Eisenhower, George Humphrey, George Allen, and unidentified United States military generals visiting Greenwood.

did.unittitle: Horse carriages and hunting, with unidentified people
Major Hole hunting party at Home Place property
Whitney, Betsey Cushing, holding a dog
Whitney, Helen and Payne, portrait at Greenwood Plantation
Whitney, John Hay and Peter di Bonaventura at Thomasville, Georgia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The photographs document dogs, wagons, countryside views, guests, and hunting.

did.unittitle: Whitney, John Hay at Thomasville, negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The guest books include photographs of visitors, and visitor notes which often contain illustrations, poems, or personal messages. Frequent visitors who signed the guest books include the Laughlin family, Hal Price Headley, the Payson family (including Joan Whitney Payson and Sandra Payson), the Paley family, the Roosevelt family (including Sara Roosevelt and Kate Roosevelt), Dwight D. Eisenhower, the Selznick family, The di Bonaventura family (including Anthony di Bonaventura and Chris di Bonaventura), and Fred Astaire.

did.unittitle: Family photo album prepared by the International Book Society showing the Whitneys hunting at Greenwood
Greenwood Plantation guest book with photographs
Greenwood Plantation guest book with photographs
Loose photographs removed from guest book
Scrapbook, Historical and Beautiful Greenwood Home of Mrs. Payne Whitney

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains class photo of John Hay Whitney's father, Payne Whitney, Groton School class of 1894.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Greenwood Seed Company was financed through Greenwood Plantation and permitted the use of land on the property for experimental hybrid corn production. The Greenwood Seed Company provided hybrid seed to other growers in the South. It initially operated under the management of Major Louis A. Beard. After Beard died in 1954, E. V. and Roy Komarek assumed managerial roles.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series contains business records of Greenwood Seed Company, including meeting minutes from the board of directors, trademark documentation, and contracts with seed companies, referred to in the business records as jobbers. Files documenting payroll and tax information are under Series II: Financial Records, and were grouped together with Greenwood Plantation finances because processing staff were not always able to ascertain whether financial records pertained to the plantation or the seed company.

did.unittitle: Bill of sale agreement between Seed Development Company and Greenwood Seed Company
Cancelled promissory notes and correspondence about capital changes for Greenwood Seed Company
Coker's Pedigreed Seed Company correspondence regarding discontinuation of work at Greenwood
Contracts to other plantations regarding the purchase of Louis A. Beard's hybrid corn seed business

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contracts are addressed to the following recipients: C. Mahlon Cline of Tallahassee Plantation; Richard K. Mellon of Pineland Plantation; Mrs. Parker Poe of Pebble Hill Plantation; and H.P. Headley of Pinebloom Plantation.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Binders of invoices and correspondence related to seed orders. The binder containers were removed during processing but original order within the binders is maintained.

did.unittitle: General seed sales, delivery schedules, and contract lists
Advertising booklet for Kernel Greenwood Hybrid Seeds

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes a flyer, contest rules booklet, and news articles regarding the history of "De Corn Weevil" song with original lyrics.

title.part: Celebrate Ourselves: The Heritage Foundation, Inc. Thomasville, Georgia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes records regarding a science fair sponsored by Greenwood Plantation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping includes materials related to a youth camp held on the grounds of Greenwood Plantation.

did.unittitle: Thomasville, Georgia: A Place Apart, published by the Thomas Country Historical Society
